notes: Plaintiff opening turn work notes. Opportunity openings:plaintiff. Objective: opening statement for proposition that by 2025-06-15 23:59 ET, Iran and the United States agreed to a permanent peace deal. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Current admitted record contains only ev_f1a176053ed8_rules, rules.txt, SHA-256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca, 1569 bytes, case_packet. Full contents read. Rules define qualifying permanent peace deal as agreement explicitly indicating military hostilities between U.S. and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or equivalent lasting-end language. Temporary agreements, negotiations, progress statements, and non-definitive announcements do not qualify. Qualifying agreement established by formal/written adoption by both sides or clear public confirmation by both governments. Primary source class: official U.S. and Iranian government information; credible reporting may also be used. Source map: decisive elements are deadline, parties, permanence/lasting cessation of military hostilities, and adoption/confirmation by both governments. Strongest expected defense: public material shows talks/proposals or cancellation/suspension around June 15, and later ceasefire/interim arrangements, not a permanent deal by deadline. Native investigation performed via web searches: site:whitehouse.gov Iran United States permanent peace deal June 15 2025; site:state.gov Iran United States peace deal June 15 2025; Iran United States agreed permanent peace deal June 15 2025; Iran US peace deal June 15 2025; June 15 2025 Iran US talks Oman suspended official Iran foreign ministry; June 15 2025 sixth round US Iran talks Oman cancelled Araghchi official; site:mfa.gov.ir June 15 2025 US Iran talks Oman; site:irna.ir June 15 2025 Iran US talks Oman. Leads located for later evidence phase: White House NSPM-2 Feb. 2025 policy context; State Department briefings June 20/26/30 2025 possible U.S. stance; IRNA/MFA pages on June 2025 talks and Iran positions; Al Jazeera reporting Araghchi confirmed June 15 Oman talks would not go ahead after Israeli attacks; ABC reporting sixth round had been scheduled for June 15; IRNA reports on Iran conditions/rejection/status. These are leads only, not record evidence. Unresolved gap: no merits evidence currently admitted proving or disproving the proposition. Opening should not claim any outside source as record proof. Durable notes saved to /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Filing plan: state the plaintiff burden under rules; frame the real dispute around whether official/written or clear bilateral confirmation existed by deadline; acknowledge current record contains only rules; identify source targets for next phase and what would satisfy/defeat burden.

Opening opportunity notes, defendant, arb-ex13-20260630163002.

Opportunity: openings:defendant. Final action allowed: record_opening_statement. Evidence submission not allowed.

Record check: get_case/list_evidence show only one juror-visible case packet exhibit: ev_f1a176053ed8_rules, sha256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca, rules.txt. Plaintiff opening is in record but plaintiff submitted no evidence.

Rules read via stat/read_evidence_range. Relevant rule contents: proposition is demonstrated only if by June 15, 2025 11:59 PM ET Iran and U.S. agreed to a permanent peace deal. Permanent peace deal must explicitly indicate U.S.-Iran military hostilities have ended or will permanently cease, or equivalent lasting-end language. Temporary arrangements and non-definitive statements do not qualify. Establishment requires either both sides sign/formally adopt a written qualifying agreement or both governments clearly and publicly confirm that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Official U.S. and Iranian government information are primary sources; credible reporting can also be used.

Source map: decisive elements are parties, deadline, written adoption or matched public confirmations, permanence as to military hostilities. Non-qualifiers: negotiation/progress statements, proposals, cancellation/suspension of talks, nuclear-only bargaining position, temporary ceasefire, later agreement after deadline.

Native investigation performed before opening: web searches for June 15 2025 U.S.-Iran permanent peace deal official statement; domain searches on WhiteHouse.gov and State.gov; searches for Iranian MFA/Araghchi official statements around June 15. Leads found include official U.S. pages suggesting continued nuclear/deal posture after the deadline and a 2026 White House statement characterizing June 2025 as refusal/followed by Operation Midnight Hammer, plus Iranian MFA lead saying June 15 was a planned meeting to craft an agreement and diplomacy was ongoing/attacked. State.gov June 26 briefing page was returned by search but direct fetch had technical difficulties/forbidden. Secondary leads include Al Jazeera reporting cancellation of the June 15 talks and later/interim 2026 deal materials. None of this is admitted evidence yet, so opening should not cite these sources as record proof. Preserve for later evidence phase.

Theory for opening: current admitted evidence proves only the governing definition and burden. Plaintiff must later supply before-deadline official bilateral adoption or matched confirmations of a permanent end to U.S.-Iran military hostilities. Defense will argue absence of that bridge; council should not let the plaintiff satisfy burden with source-chain fragments, negotiations, proposals, unilateral statements, temporary pauses, later instruments, or nuclear-program-only material. Provenance/custody matter because the dispositive proof is a formal sovereign act or public confirmation from both governments.

Unresolved gaps: Need later submission of captured primary/credible sources before relying on them. Later evidence targets: official White House/State, Iranian MFA/leader/president pages, mediator materials from Oman/Pakistan, archived State pages, and credible reporting that links back to primary texts.

Plaintiff merits turn notes. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Before plaintiff submissions, only ev_f1a176053ed8_rules existed; defendant opening added no evidence. I investigated official/credible source targets from opening source map. Opened White House NSPM-2 page: official U.S. maximum-pressure policy against Iran in Feb. 2025, no peace agreement confirmation. Opened IRNA News ID 85858952 from Jun. 11, 2025: Araqchi says agreement with U.S. within reach if enrichment and sanctions conditions are met, sixth round scheduled for Jun. 15; this supports negotiations/conditions, not completed deal. Opened IRNA News ID 85862430 from Jun. 14, 2025: Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson said Iran had not decided on attending next indirect talks and said negotiations effectively rendered meaningless; this is adverse to proposition. Opened Al Jazeera Jun. 14, 2025 article: reports Araghchi confirmed Sunday Oman talks cancelled, Oman also confirmed, senior U.S. official confirmed talks would not happen while U.S. remained committed to talks; secondary source but directly relevant. ABC Jun. 12, 2025 scheduling report found as lead but not submitted because IRNA/Al Jazeera preserve same scheduling/cancellation sequence sufficiently. Submitted evidence accepted: ev_ec1077ef26bb_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-ec1077ef26bb (White House NSPM-2 capture); ev_ad54253d5a07_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-ad54253d5a07 (IRNA within reach/conditions); ev_20710444fe69_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-20710444fe69 (IRNA undecided/meaningless talks); ev_e3dbc948dd66_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-e3dbc948dd66 (Al Jazeera cancellation). Self-audit: source trail does not establish a written/formally adopted agreement or clear bilateral public confirmation by both governments that a permanent peace deal had been definitively established by deadline. It shows maximum pressure, conditional negotiations, uncertainty, and cancellation. Provenance/custody: White House official U.S.; IRNA Iranian state media reporting official Iranian positions; Al Jazeera credible secondary but not primary. Filing decision: As plaintiff, make candid merits submission; offer rules plus submitted evidence; explain plaintiff cannot responsibly claim preponderance on current record and identify missing links. Durable notes updated in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits opportunity notes, arb-ex13-20260630163002.

Opportunity: arguments:defendant. Final action allowed: submit_argument. Evidence submission allowed.

Fresh record scan: Plaintiff submitted four evidence captures and a technical source ledger. Plaintiff's own merits argument states that the record does not meet plaintiff's burden and that proposition has not been demonstrated.

Evidence inspected/read:
- ev_f1a176053ed8_rules: governing rule. Qualifying deal requires written adoption or matched official public confirmation, and permanent cessation of U.S.-Iran military hostilities. Temporary/non-definitive statements excluded.
- ev_ec1077ef26bb_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-ec1077ef26bb: White House NSPM-2 maximum pressure policy. It does not contain U.S.-Iran peace agreement or confirmation.
- ev_ad54253d5a07_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-ad54253d5a07: IRNA 2025-06-11; agreement with U.S. was "within reach" if enrichment/sanctions conditions met; sixth round scheduled for June 15. Shows negotiation/conditional possibility, not completed permanent peace.
- ev_20710444fe69_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-20710444fe69: IRNA 2025-06-14; Iran had not decided whether to attend next U.S. talks and spokesperson said negotiations were effectively rendered meaningless. Strong negative evidence.
- ev_e3dbc948dd66_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-e3dbc948dd66: Al Jazeera 2025-06-14; Araghchi confirmed next Oman talks cancelled; Oman and U.S. official context. Secondary but corroborates cancellation and absence of completed deal.

Defense evidence submitted and accepted:
- ev_ba131554a9d3_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-ba131554a9d3: official Iranian MFA 2025-06-20 Araghchi UNHRC address. Relevant line says Iran was in an ongoing diplomatic process and was supposed to meet Americans on June 15 to craft a promising agreement for peaceful resolution of nuclear-program issues. This is official Iranian evidence that the alleged agreement was not completed by June 15.
- ev_66cd7f8ab87c_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-66cd7f8ab87c: official White House 2026-02-06 fact sheet. It says in June, after Iran refused to reach a deal with the United States, President Trump authorized Operation Midnight Hammer. Later official U.S. characterization is inconsistent with a completed pre-deadline permanent peace deal.

Adverse/plaintiff theory check: Plaintiff might point to "within reach," "peaceful resolution," or general deal language. Under rules, those are prospective/conditional and nuclear-issue specific, not a written agreement or matched public confirmation of permanent cessation of military hostilities.

Planned filing: Offer rules, plaintiff exhibits PX-2 through PX-5, and defense exhibits D-1/D-2. Argue that the full record, including plaintiff's concession, affirmatively defeats the proposition by showing negotiations/cancellation/refusal rather than a qualifying sovereign act. Include technical report summarizing search/custody and remaining gaps.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Defendant added two evidence items: ev_ba131554a9d3_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-ba131554a9d3 (Iran MFA Araghchi June 20 address) and ev_66cd7f8ab87c_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-66cd7f8ab87c (White House Feb. 2026 fact sheet). I used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for both. Exact read of Iran MFA item confirms Araghchi said Iran was attacked in the midst of an ongoing diplomatic process and was supposed to meet Americans on June 15 to craft a promising agreement for peaceful resolution of nuclear-program issues. That supports prospective negotiations, not an already completed permanent U.S.-Iran peace deal by deadline. Exact read of White House item confirms later official U.S. characterization: in June, after Iran refused to reach a deal with the United States, Trump authorized Operation Midnight Hammer; the item does not identify a completed June 15 permanent peace deal. Analysis: defendant's new official evidence reinforces the plaintiff's prior candid merits submission and does not create a useful factual rebuttal. No further source capture needed. Durable journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Decision: pass rebuttal phase to avoid needless noise.

Defendant surrebuttal opportunity notes, arb-ex13-20260630163002.

Opportunity: surrebuttals:defendant. Allowed final actions: submit_surrebuttal or pass_phase_opportunity.

Fresh record and evidence scan performed via get_case and list_evidence. Evidence list shows no new evidence after the defendant merits argument: rules exhibit, four plaintiff submissions, and two defendant submissions. Rebuttals list is empty in get_case; plaintiff appears not to have filed substantive rebuttal text or rebuttal exhibits.

Analysis: There is no new rebuttal point, quote, exhibit, source-chain claim, or metadata issue to answer. Existing defendant merits argument already addresses the strongest contrary point: conditional/prospective "within reach" and "craft agreement" language is not a written adoption or matched public confirmation of permanent U.S.-Iran military-hostilities peace.

Decision: pass the surrebuttal opportunity rather than repeat prior argument. No additional native investigation or evidence submission needed because no new factual issue was raised.

Plaintiff closing notes. Opportunity closings:plaintiff. Final record scan performed with get_case, list_evidence, and stat_evidence for rules plus key defendant official items. Closing phase does not allow new evidence. Record consists of rules, plaintiff submitted White House/IRNA/Al Jazeera captures, defendant Iran MFA and White House captures, and technical reports. Analysis: rules require written/formal adoption by both governments or clear public confirmation by both governments of a qualifying agreement definitively established by June 15 2025 23:59 ET, with permanent cessation of U.S.-Iran military hostilities. Evidence instead shows U.S. maximum-pressure policy, Iranian conditional negotiations, uncertainty/cancellation around June 15, official Iranian later description that June 15 meeting was to craft an agreement during ongoing diplomacy, and official U.S. later statement that Iran refused to reach a deal in June. Missing links: no written peace instrument, no formal adoption, no matched official confirmations, no definitive establishment by both governments, no permanent hostilities-cessation language. Provenance/custody: strongest evidence is official or state-linked and converges against proposition. Closing decision: be candid and ask council to resolve not demonstrated. Durable notes updated in /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant closing opportunity notes, arb-ex13-20260630163002.

Opportunity: closings:defendant. Final action allowed: deliver_closing_statement. No evidence submission or reports allowed.

Fresh record/evidence scan: get_case/list_evidence/stat performed. No new evidence since arguments. Plaintiff closing explicitly argues that the proposition should resolve not demonstrated, using the same evidentiary defects the defense identified.

Evidence analysis: D-1/rules define strict requirement: written adoption by both governments or matched official public confirmation of a qualifying agreement, plus permanent cessation of U.S.-Iran military hostilities. D-2 White House maximum pressure policy does not confirm peace. D-3 IRNA June 11 is conditional and prospective: agreement within reach if conditions met, with talks scheduled for June 15. D-4 IRNA June 14 says Iran had not decided whether to attend talks and negotiations were effectively rendered meaningless. D-5 Al Jazeera secondary report corroborates cancellation of the June 15 talks with Iran/Oman/U.S. official context. D-6 official Iran MFA says the June 15 meeting was supposed to craft an agreement during an ongoing process. D-7 official White House later says Iran refused to reach a deal in June before Operation Midnight Hammer.

Strongest contrary point: 'within reach' and 'peaceful resolution' language. It fails because it is prospective, conditional, nuclear-program focused, and not a written adoption or matched public confirmation of permanent hostilities cessation.

Closing plan: concise synthesis; both sides now effectively agree the burden is not met; ask council to resolve not demonstrated.
